rag_rules: Use AGREE syntax: assume/guarantee for contracts, eq for equations, const for constants, and assign for assignments
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if...then...else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contract expressions
For data constraints, use guarantees with logical equivalence (=> and <=>) when appropriate
generic_rules: Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope
Avoid inventing declarations for unresolved references; focus on existing architecture
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees only when concrete bounds are available
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use '->' for temporal recurrence in stateful equations
Use 'if...then...else' for branching logic in equations
anti_patterns: Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not invent declarations for unresolved references; use existing visible identifiers
Do not use AADL property names (Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, etc.) as AGREE variables
